name: Muslim Academy of Greater Orlando
mission: The mission of the Muslim Academy of Greater Orlando is to provide quality education of the highest standard in a safe, supportive, dynamic, and engaging Islamic environment so that students have the opportunity to excel to their fullest potential.
theoryOfChange: By providing a dynamic student-centered educational environment with high academic standards and expectations, Islamic values and morals, students will develop a sense of purpose, a connection to ALLAH, and a desire to serve their creator via service to others and their community.
scoreSummary: Muslim Academy of Greater Orlando Inc shows moderate impact and moderate alignment (significant risk deductions applied).

dimension_explanations.alignment: With an alignment score of 24/50, the organization addresses the urgent cause of global education but shows a lower fit for donors seeking high-growth or high-directness interventions [6][7]. As a Sadaqah-eligible institution, it provides a consistent track record for general charitable giving within the Florida educational landscape [8].
dimension_explanations.impact: The academy focuses on capacity building through its Pre-K and K-8 programs, though its impact score of 27/50 reflects a lack of specific cost-per-beneficiary data [3][5]. While the organization demonstrates financial health with 15.6 months of working capital, the program expense ratio stands at 67.8%, indicating that a significant portion of funds is directed toward administrative or secondary costs [1][2].

strengths.detail: The academy maintains <cite id="9">15.6 months of working capital</cite>, providing a massive safety net for long-term operational stability.
The organization spends <cite id="8">zero dollars on fundraising expenses</cite>, ensuring maximum resource allocation to educational services.

strengths_deep_dive: The Muslim Academy of Greater Orlando demonstrates remarkable financial resilience, evidenced by its <cite id="9">15.6 months of operating reserves</cite>. This level of liquidity is significantly higher than the industry standard, allowing the school to weather economic shifts without compromising student services. Furthermore, the school has achieved a <cite id="11">3-year revenue CAGR of 25.5%</cite>, signaling rapid growth and increasing community trust. Its academic standing is bolstered by its <cite id="6">A+ Niche grade</cite>, which reflects high standards in teacher quality and student outcomes.
rich_narrative.summary: The Muslim Academy of Greater Orlando (MAGO) operates as a faith-based educational institution that has served the Florida community since <cite id="1">its founding in 2006</cite>. The academy provides a structured learning environment for students from <cite id="2">Pre-K through 8th Grade</cite>, focusing on a curriculum that blends academic excellence with spiritual growth. Financial data from 2024 shows the organization manages <cite id="3">$3,572,587 in total revenue</cite>, reflecting significant growth in its operational capacity. While the school primarily generates income through contributions, it also maintains <cite id="4">$479,477 in program service revenue</cite>, indicating a diversified funding model that includes tuition and fees. The institution is <cite id="5">accredited by Cognia</cite> and has earned recognition as an <cite id="6">A+ rated school by Niche</cite>, placing it among the top-tier private educational options in the region. MAGO emphasizes a mission of <cite id="7">virtue, wisdom, and strength</cite>, aiming to develop well-rounded individuals who are prepared for both higher education and community leadership. Despite its strong local reputation, the organization maintains a lean administrative profile, reporting <cite id="8">$0.00 spent to raise every $1</cite> in its most recent filings. This high fundraising efficiency ensures that donor contributions are utilized directly for school operations and facility maintenance.
